mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation pressure, as measured by year-over-year changes in CPIAUCSL, forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or accelerate monetary tightening, raising short-term rate expectations and pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 2-year Rank IC of CPIAUCSL changes with 20-day forward yield changes is below 0.03, or the correlation is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, the labor market becomes extremely tight. Subsequent further declines in UNRATE amplify wage inflation fears, driving long-term yields upward within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE is below 4.0, the 20-day forward yield direction is negative more than 55% of the time, or the average yield change does not exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signals robust aggregate demand. Strong demand pressures raise real rate expectations and term premiums, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal (INDPRO and PAYEMS both above trend) fails to produce positive mean 20-day yield changes, or the interaction term in a regression has a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime (CPI YoY > 5%), any further acceleration in CPIAUCSL intensifies Fed tightening expectations, resulting in an unambiguous positive drift in the 10-year yield over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Within the regime defined by CPI YoY > 5%, the 20-day forward yield change is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a strong economy, which supports higher neutral interest rates and pushes the 10-year yield upward over a quarterly horizon as the data is released and revised.
failure_condition: The quarterly correlation between GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60‑day yield changes is not significantly positive, or the sign is negative in >40% of rolling 10‑year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1.5 million units signals strong housing demand and construction activity. This real economic momentum translates into higher long-term interest rates as investors price in stronger growth and potential inflation over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1500, the average 20‑day yield change is negative or not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and industrial production indicates broad-based demand momentum. The interaction of these two demand proxies amplifies the signal for rising yields as the economy operates above potential, causing the 10-year yield to rise within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The product of detrended RSAFS and INDPRO has no significant positive correlation with 5-day forward yield changes, or the interaction term in a linear model is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, declines in the federal funds rate reduce the entire yield curve as monetary policy loosens. Falling short-term rates directly lower the 10-year yield through expectations and term premium compression over a 20-trading-day horizon.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle (FEDFUNDS declining over 3 months)
failure_condition: Periods identified as easing cycles produce 20‑day yield increases more than half the time, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and yield changes is not positive (since easing lowers rates).
